revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:一ノ矢八坂神社]]
{{Infobox religious building |name = 一ノ矢八坂神社 |image = File:Haiden of Ichinoya Yasaka Shrine.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:茨城県|茨城県]][[:en:つくば市|つくば市]][[玉取 (つくば市)|玉取]]2617 |位置 = {{coord|36|7|28.9|N|140|5|52.1|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧郷社{{sfn|大穂町史編纂委員会 編|1989|p=275}} |創建 = <sub>伝</sub>[[Jōgan]]元年（[[859]]）{{sfn|佐野|1980|p=40}} |宮司 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]] |別名 = 一ノ矢の天王様{{sfn|大穂町史編纂委員会 編|1989|p=275}}{{sfn|「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会 編|1983|p=1253}}{{sfn|山崎|2002|p=185}}、一ノ矢天王宮{{sfn|平凡社|1982|p=572}} |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|June 7 (Lunisolar)|ja|6月7日 (旧暦)}}（{{ill|Gion and Tenno Festivals|ja|祇園祭|lt=Gion and Tenno Festival}}）{{sfn|佐野|1980|p=41}}{{sfn|「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会 編|1983|p=1253}} |神事 = [[元旦祭]]、[[節分祭]]など{{sfn|平凡社|1982|p=572}} |地図 = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=7|lat_sec=28.9|lon_deg=140|lon_min=5|lon_sec=52.1|label=一ノ矢八坂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} |location = {{ill|Tsukuba|qid=Q273863|1=Tsukuba}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.124694|N|140.097806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Ichinoya Yasaka Shrine''' (一ノ矢八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tamatari, Tsukuba|ja|玉取 (つくば市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatari, Tsukuba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tsukuba|en|Tsukuba|qid=Q273863|lt=Tsukuba|1=Tsukuba}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, Japan. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. The shrine hosts the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|qid=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival|1=Gion Matsuri}}, commonly known as the "Garlic Festival" (ニンニク祭り).
The name of the nearby {{ill|Ichinoya Student Dormitory|ja|筑波大学筑波キャンパス#一の矢学生宿舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinoya Student Dormitory|1=University of Tsukuba, Tsukuba Campus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11602854}} at {{ill|University of Tsukuba|en|University of Tsukuba|qid=Q743615|lt=University of Tsukuba|1=University of Tsukuba}} is derived from this shrine.
== History == The shrine is said to have been founded in the first year of the {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan era|1=Jōgan}} (859) during the reign of Emperor Seiwa. According to one theory, it was established as a branch of the {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} in {{ill|Atago District, Yamashiro Province|ja|愛宕郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago District, Yamashiro Province|1=Otagi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10981345}} (present-day {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}). However, the "Ōho Town History" notes that the oldest artifact at the shrine dates back only to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|qid=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, leaving earlier origins uncertain.
In the {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|qid=Q1327340|lt=Tengyō era|1=Tengyō}} (938–947), {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Hidesato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1136623}} visited the shrine to pray for victory over {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|qid=Q1207652|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|qid=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicating arrows as offerings.
During the height of the {{ill|Oda clan (Hitachi Province)|ja|小田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda clan (Hitachi Province)|1=Oda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11463150}}, the shrine was revered and known as the "Flower Garden of Tamatari Village." The shrine received offerings of bows and arrows, and the shrine buildings were burned during the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1573–1593) following the fall of {{ill|Oda Castle|en|Oda Castle|qid=Q11463019|lt=Oda Castle (Hitachi Province)|1=Oda Castle}}.
== Legends of the Name Ichinoya == There are legends explaining the origin of the name "Ichinoya."
{{Cquote|Long ago, crows plagued the area's crops, and villagers sought the help of master archers. The place where they gathered was called "Tenyaba." The first arrow shot down a crow at what is now "Ichinoya," the second at "Ninoya," and the third at "Sannoya." The crows carried jewels, leading to the village's name "Tamatari."}}
== Worship and Festivals == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, worshiped for protection against calamities and diseases, and maritime safety. Despite being inland, the shrine attracts many visitors involved in maritime activities.
The shrine is central to the {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} faith in Ibaraki Prefecture, and it is said that other shrines do not hold their Gion Festivals until this shrine's festival concludes. The {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|qid=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival|1=Gion Matsuri}} here is colloquially called the "Garlic Festival" due to historical practices encouraging garlic consumption to ward off illness during famines.
== Cultural Properties == The shrine houses sacred treasures such as swords and mirrors donated by the Oda clan, including the notable "{{ill|Zuihana Sōchō Hachiryōkyō|ja|瑞花雙鳥八稜鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zuihana Sōchō Hachiryōkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," a bronze mirror from the {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|qid=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku era|1=Eiroku}}.
== Transportation == * By public transport: Take a {{ill|Kantō Railway|en|Kantō Railway|qid=Q845943|lt=Kantō Railway|1=Kantō Railway}} bus from {{ill|Tsuchiura Station|en|Tsuchiura Station|qid=Q5364280|lt=Tsuchiura Station|1=Tsuchiura Station}} to "Ichinoya Intersection" or "Kurihara." * By car: Drive north on the {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural Route 55|ja|茨城県道55号土浦つくば線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Prefectural Route 55|1=Ibaraki Prefectural Road Route 55|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11617770}} and turn right at "Ichinoya Intersection."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yasaka.or.jp/ Official Website of Ichinoya Yasaka Shrine] * [http://www.ibaraki-jinjacho.jp/ibaraki/kennan/jinja/11180.html Ichinoya Yasaka Shrine - Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Association]
{{translated page|ja|一ノ矢八坂神社|version=100126373|comment=Imported full ja history}}
